<!-- generated by: reviewer | run: 2026-02-24T11:25 | evidence: specs/inbox/ stale files, spec agent daily log 2026-02-24 10:54, spec agent can_write in agents.yaml -->
# Spec Inbox Cleanup Convention

## What I'm Seeing

`specs/inbox/` currently contains `pipeline-next-step.md` and `self-review-loop.md`. Both have already been fully processed: specced, built, verified (or failed), and archived to `specs/archive/`. The Spec agent identified this staleness in its daily log (`memory/daily/spec/2026-02-24.md` at 10:54) and noted "Action needed (human or operator)" because it cannot clean up inbox.

The structural cause: when Spec picks up an intent from `specs/inbox/`, it creates a draft in `specs/drafting/` — but Spec's `can_write` list (`agents.yaml` lines 31-32) includes only `specs/drafting/` and `specs/ready/`, not `specs/inbox/`. So Spec literally cannot remove the file it consumed. The same issue affects `specs/drafting/`: after promoting to `specs/ready/`, the drafting copy was left behind. Today there are also stale copies of both specs in `specs/drafting/`.

The `spec-lifecycle` convention (`specs/archive/spec-lifecycle.md`) governs the `ready/` → `archive/` transition but says nothing about `inbox/` → `drafting/` or `drafting/` → `ready/` cleanup.

This violates the value "The environment is the product" (`universe/values.md`) — stale files create ambiguous state about what's been processed and what hasn't.

## What I Want to See

A defined convention for the full spec lifecycle from inbox through archive, with either: (a) Spec agent's `can_write` expanded to include `specs/inbox/` so it can clean up consumed intents, or (b) Operator responsibility to clean inbox/drafting after specs reach archive, or (c) some other mechanism. The four stale files should be removed as part of establishing whichever convention is chosen.
